result:
Cleaned up. The dev server is stopped and everything else is untouched.

## Summary

The site was mid-pivot: product changed from "Lumina" (meeting-notes AI) to "InboxZap" (email cleanup), and the migration was left half-done. That explains the GSC pattern — homepage ranks fine (position 4.5) and the branded query `inboxzap` performs well, but the blog (12 URLs, ~13,900 combined monthly impressions) sits at position 40–58 with near-zero CTR, and ~30 matching informational queries (inbox zero, bulk delete emails, gmail storage, unsubscribe, filters, etc.) show real volume going nowhere.

Root causes found and fixed:
- **Homepage copy still described the old product** while `<head>` metadata described the new one — rewrote it to match, added an FAQ section.
- **Blog was completely broken**: a client-only component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist anywhere in the repo. Rebuilt as server-rendered pages with real, unique content for all 12 posts (kept the original `post-1`…`post-12` URLs to preserve existing search equity).
- **Sitemap excluded all blog posts** and listed `/features`/`/pricing`, which didn't exist as pages — built those pages for real and fixed the sitemap to enumerate everything dynamically.
- **`/faq` permanently redirected to a `#faq` anchor that didn't exist** — added it.
- **Broken OG image reference** — added a valid placeholder asset and fixed `metadataBase`.
- **No structured data** — added Organization/WebSite/BlogPosting JSON-LD (deliberately skipped FAQPage schema, which is restricted to gov/health sites).

Verified with a full production build and by curling every route on a local server — all 200s/expected redirects, correct titles/metadata/JSON-LD.

Full findings and evidence are in `FULL-AUDIT-REPORT.md`, prioritized fixes and honest follow-ups (blog posts are ~450–950 words, short of the 1,500-word quality gate; `next@14.2.5` has a disclosed CVE) are in `ACTION-PLAN.md`, both in the repo root.
